what: M=64 fused path: keep 16x128x256 tile but reduce num_warps from 4 to 2
why: The current best still has the 64x7168x2048 shape as one of the largest contributors. Prior NW=2 regressions used different BSN=64 or autotuned tile families; this isolates num_warps=2 on the current v211 16x128x256 path to test whether lower warp count reduces register pressure without changing grid shape or pipelining.
override: A nearly identical idea was correctness-tested earlier but never benchmarked or leaderboarded from this session. I am carrying it forward as a fresh measured run on the current baseline.

what: Split-K path uses BLOCK_SIZE_K=512 with NUM_KSPLIT=2
why: The 16x2112x7168 shape pays both GEMM and reduction cost. The current 7-way split maximizes grid size but also maximizes partial reduction work. A 2-way split with larger K tiles cuts the reduction burden sharply while leaving every other shape on the current winning baseline. A prior branch test-passed this configuration, so it is a valid candidate for benchmark measurement.
override: Earlier split-K experiments covered many other points, but this exact split-K=2 plus BSK=512 combination has not been benchmarked from the current active session.

what: Split-K path uses BLOCK_SIZE_K=512 with NUM_KSPLIT=2 and num_stages=1
why: Once the split-K path is reduced to only two partials, the balance changes: reduction overhead falls, but each split now runs a long K loop with a large 512 tile. The baseline split-7 winner needs num_stages=2 for BSK=256, but this 2-way / BSK=512 case may benefit from less pipelining pressure and lower register usage.
override: This is a new combination: prior BSK512 work either used different split counts or different tuned-library settings. The exact split-K=2 plus num_stages=1 point has not been measured.
